sn22.156:1.1: At Sāvatthī. "Bhikkhus, when what exists, by clinging to what, by adhering to what, does view of self arise?"[n.236] Attānudiṭṭhi. At Paṭis I 143 defined by the formula for the twenty kinds of sakkāyadiṭṭhi.
sn22.156:1.2: "Venerable sir, our teachings are rooted in the Blessed One … ."
sn22.156:1.3: "When there is form, bhikkhus, by clinging to form, by adhering to form, view of self arises. sn.iii.186 When there is feeling … perception … volitional formations … consciousness, by clinging to consciousness, by adhering to consciousness, view of self arises." …
sn22.156:1.4: "Seeing thus … He understands: ‘ … there is no more for this state of being.’"
